claim_boundary: Passing this gate supports a narrow revolutionary computational-substrate candidate: exact repeated impact intelligence can move from repeated recomputation to shared immutable snapshots. It does not prove revenue, user demand, production reliability, or universal process acceleration.
do_not_build: A per-card point-query API as the main product surface.
A cold database lookup path for every consumer request.
A one-off CI test-skipping product before the impact cards are validated by users.

negative_controls.claim_boundary: Expected failure: low request counts do not amortize card setup enough for 1000x.
negative_controls.name: low_request_wall_clock

negative_controls.claim_boundary: Expected failure: uncached SQLite point lookups are not the revolutionary path.
negative_controls.name: sqlite_point_lookup

negative_controls.claim_boundary: Expected failure: chatty point HTTP requests erase most of the advantage.
negative_controls.name: point_http_api

negative_controls.claim_boundary: Expected failure: simple repository bundles still issue too many network requests.
negative_controls.name: bundle_http_api

next_required_proofs: Replace the generated GitHub issue/Actions/Pages proof service with an owned deployed endpoint, durable production queue, managed tenant database, observability, and SLOs.
Validate the cards against real security, release, compliance, or ownership workflows with target users.
Prove actual third-party/customer issue or webhook requests, not only requests opened with a token controlled by our own account.

Run trace-generated substrate discovery on real customer workflow telemetry or full upstream suites; the hosted upstream-pytest proof uses actual call events from 24 upstream Click tests, but it is still a curated subset and an attrs AST-history probe did not clear 1000x.

north_star.one_sentence: Decide whether the impact-pointer work has earned the narrower claim of a revolutionary computational substrate candidate.
north_star.why_this_moves_us_closer: It joins independent positive evidence with falsifying negative controls and refuses to blur computational evidence into business proof.

positive_evidence.claim_boundary: Formal CODEOWNERS-routed operation-count workflow across distinct external repositories.
positive_evidence.name: formal_owner_blast_radius_portfolio

positive_evidence.claim_boundary: Behavioral owner-signal validation from sampled merged GitHub PRs, not causal proof.
positive_evidence.name: pr_review_owner_signal_validation

positive_evidence.claim_boundary: Fully measured repeated-card consumer wall-clock benchmark on two external repositories.
positive_evidence.name: repeated_consumer_wall_clock_portfolio

positive_evidence.claim_boundary: Local SQLite-backed store hydrated into a hot in-process cache.
positive_evidence.name: persistent_hydrated_card_service

positive_evidence.claim_boundary: Local HTTP immutable snapshot plus local fanout, not deployed auth/TLS/multi-tenant service.
positive_evidence.name: local_http_snapshot_fanout

positive_evidence.claim_boundary: Local HTTP tenant snapshot with HMAC request signing, content-address verification, wrong-secret denial, and wrong-tenant isolation.
positive_evidence.name: authenticated_tenant_snapshot_service

positive_evidence.claim_boundary: Authenticated tenant snapshot with injected one-time response latency; this is a local delay stress test, not deployed internet/TLS measurement.
positive_evidence.name: authenticated_snapshot_latency_stress

positive_evidence.claim_boundary: Changed already-compiled impact-card payload published as a new signed immutable snapshot while the old content address remains fetchable.
positive_evidence.name: authenticated_snapshot_update_latency

positive_evidence.claim_boundary: Historical pull-request-like commit event recomputed into a matching CODEOWNERS-routed card and published into a signed immutable snapshot from warm replay/index state.
positive_evidence.name: authenticated_snapshot_webhook_recompute

positive_evidence.claim_boundary: Replicated historical pull-request-like recompute events across multiple external repositories, from warm replay/index state into signed immutable snapshots.
positive_evidence.name: authenticated_snapshot_webhook_recompute_portfolio

positive_evidence.claim_boundary: Local GitHub push-shaped HMAC webhook POST validates changed files, recomputes historical cards, persists snapshots and webhook events in SQLite, rejects a bad webhook signature, and returns signed tenant snapshots.
positive_evidence.name: authenticated_snapshot_http_webhook_durable

positive_evidence.claim_boundary: Live GitHub PR metadata anchors the formal-owner card merge commits and Python source-root changed files across the six-repository portfolio.
positive_evidence.name: live_github_pr_card_anchor_portfolio

positive_evidence.claim_boundary: Live GitHub API PR metadata anchors the merge commits and source-root changed files before a local signed webhook recomputes historical cards into durable signed tenant snapshots.
positive_evidence.name: authenticated_snapshot_live_github_pr_durable

positive_evidence.claim_boundary: A generated private GitHub repository ran the live-PR, HMAC webhook, SQLite snapshot, digest fetch, and bad-signature proof on GitHub-hosted Actions.
positive_evidence.name: github_actions_external_runner_proof

positive_evidence.claim_boundary: A generated private GitHub repository received a signed repository_dispatch event via GitHub's public API, validated the client payload signature on a hosted runner, then ran the live-PR, local HMAC webhook, SQLite snapshot, digest fetch, and bad-signature proof.
positive_evidence.name: github_repository_dispatch_public_ingress_proof

positive_evidence.claim_boundary: GitHub delivered an actual signed push webhook over the public internet through a temporary TLS tunnel to the snapshot handler; the handler verified GitHub HMAC, verified a signed trigger payload, fetched live PR metadata, stored local SQLite snapshots, rejected a bad public signature, and served signed snapshots back through the tunnel.
positive_evidence.name: github_webhook_tunnel_delivery_proof

positive_evidence.claim_boundary: GitHub delivered an actual signed push webhook over the public internet to the handler; the handler verified GitHub HMAC and a signed trigger payload, fetched live PR metadata, persisted content-addressed snapshots into a separate private GitHub repository, rejected a bad public signature, and verified stored snapshot contents through GitHub's Contents API.
positive_evidence.name: github_webhook_managed_storage_proof

positive_evidence.claim_boundary: A GitHub-hosted Actions runner independently checked the managed storage commit, event record, content-addressed snapshot files, and live PR anchors from the generated storage repository, then proved it detects tampered snapshot, event, and live-PR expectations.
positive_evidence.name: github_managed_storage_external_verifier_proof

positive_evidence.claim_boundary: A public generated GitHub repository exposes the immutable snapshot/event artifacts. A GitHub-hosted runner verifies them through unauthenticated raw.githubusercontent.com HTTPS fetches, live PR anchors, and tamper-detection controls.
positive_evidence.name: github_public_https_snapshot_proof

positive_evidence.claim_boundary: A separate public GitHub auditor repository fetches the public artifact repository's raw HTTPS snapshot URLs by commit, verifies digests and live PR anchors, and proves tamper detection from a distinct hosted workflow.
positive_evidence.name: github_public_cross_repo_auditor_proof

positive_evidence.claim_boundary: A separate public GitHub-hosted consumer fully measured one public HTTPS snapshot fetch plus local fanout for 10,000 reads, sampled naive repeated public point fetches, projected the same request count, and showed the low-request negative control fails 1000x.
positive_evidence.name: github_public_consumer_benchmark_proof

positive_evidence.claim_boundary: A GitHub Pages hosted static HTTPS endpoint serves immutable snapshots; a separate public verifier repository measured one-fetch fanout, sampled and projected repeated Pages point fetches, and showed the low-request negative control fails 1000x.
positive_evidence.name: github_pages_snapshot_service_proof

positive_evidence.claim_boundary: A public generated GitHub service repository accepted signed repository_dispatch ingress, used hosted Actions compute plus git-backed storage to publish snapshots, served them through GitHub Pages HTTPS, and rejected a bad-signature dispatch on the hosted runner.
positive_evidence.name: github_dispatch_pages_dynamic_service_proof

positive_evidence.claim_boundary: A public generated GitHub service repository accepted a signed issue opened through the public Issues surface, used hosted Actions compute plus git-backed storage to publish snapshots, served them through GitHub Pages HTTPS, and rejected a bad-signature issue on the hosted runner.
positive_evidence.name: github_issue_pages_public_ingress_proof

positive_evidence.claim_boundary: A separate public client repository's hosted workflow opened signed public issues in a public service repository, waited for hosted service workflows, verified GitHub Pages snapshots, and proved the bad-signature service run failed.
positive_evidence.name: github_cross_repo_issue_client_proof

positive_evidence.claim_boundary: A bounded miner generated join/index substrate candidates from raw relation traces, selected the symbol reverse-index substrate by training plus heldout validation, included discovery/validation oracle cost, and showed the low-repeat control fails 1000x.
positive_evidence.name: raw_trace_substrate_miner

positive_evidence.claim_boundary: A public LangChain git-history slice supplied real AST facts and changed-file cases; the system mined the symbol reverse-index substrate from traces, emitted content-addressed snapshots, and showed repeated raw scans lose to snapshot fanout while low-repeat demand fails 1000x.
positive_evidence.name: real_git_trace_mined_snapshot_fanout

positive_evidence.claim_boundary: A public verifier repository ran the real-git trace-mined fanout proof on a GitHub-hosted Linux runner against a fresh public LangChain sparse clone, emitted the hosted report in workflow logs, and reproduced the 1000x fanout result plus low-request negative control.
positive_evidence.name: github_real_git_trace_mined_fanout_proof

positive_evidence.claim_boundary: A public GitHub-hosted verifier ran the trace-mined fanout proof across LangChain, Click, and HTTPX fresh sparse clones, with every target selecting the generated symbol reverse-index plan, clearing 1000x, and keeping the low-request negative control below 1000x.
positive_evidence.name: github_real_git_trace_mined_fanout_portfolio_proof

positive_evidence.claim_boundary: Raw Python call events from executing public Click workflows yielded a generated file-to-workflow index, exact heldout validation, content-addressed snapshots, 1000x repeated-consumer fanout, and a low-request negative control.
positive_evidence.name: runtime_trace_mined_workflow_fanout

positive_evidence.claim_boundary: A public GitHub-hosted verifier cloned Click, executed proof workflows under sys.settrace, mined the generated file-to-workflow index from raw call events, and reproduced the 1000x snapshot fanout result on hosted infrastructure.
positive_evidence.name: github_runtime_trace_mined_workflow_fanout_proof

positive_evidence.claim_boundary: Raw Python call events from upstream-authored Click pytest cases yielded a generated file-to-upstream-test index, exact heldout validation, content-addressed snapshots, 1000x repeated-consumer fanout, and a low-request negative control.
positive_evidence.name: upstream_pytest_runtime_trace_fanout

positive_evidence.claim_boundary: A public GitHub-hosted verifier cloned Click source and tests, ran upstream-authored pytest cases under sys.settrace, mined the generated file-to-upstream-test index from raw call events, and reproduced the 1000x snapshot fanout result on hosted infrastructure.
positive_evidence.name: github_upstream_pytest_runtime_trace_fanout_proof

strongest_measured_product_shape.why_it_matters: The result survives auth, tenant routing, content-address verification, and publish accounting only when repeated consumers share an immutable snapshot. A one-second injected response delay still clears 1000x, a changed snapshot revision publishes in milliseconds, and a historical changed-file event can recompute matching cards into signed snapshots from warm state across multiple external repos. Live GitHub PR metadata anchors 30 of 31 card changed-file inputs across the six-repo portfolio. The strongest path now includes signed HTTP webhook ingestion, live GitHub PR metadata for the replayed merge commits and changed files, durable SQLite storage, digest-verified fetches, bad-signature rejection, a GitHub-hosted Actions runner proof, a signed repository_dispatch public-ingress proof, and actual GitHub push webhook delivery through a temporary public TLS tunnel with externally managed GitHub repository storage. A separate GitHub-hosted verifier now audits that storage commit, event record, snapshot digests, and live PR anchors without trusting this workstation, and its hosted negative controls prove it detects tampered snapshot, event, and live-PR expectations. A public GitHub mirror then proves the artifacts can be audited over unauthenticated raw HTTPS with the same live-anchor and tamper controls. A separate public auditor repo removes the self-auditing weakness by fetching the artifact repo's raw commit URLs from a distinct hosted workflow. A separate public consumer benchmark then measures the actual shared-artifact consumption shape: one public snapshot fetch plus local fanout clears a projected 2641x against sampled repeated public point fetches, while the 20-request negative control fails 1000x. A GitHub Pages deployment then serves the same artifact shape from an HTTPS-enforced public static endpoint; a separate public verifier measures one Pages snapshot fetch plus fanout at 0.119001 seconds and a projected 2168x speedup against sampled repeated Pages point fetches, while the low-request control remains only 4.3889x. The latest dynamic proof turns that static artifact path into a public managed pipeline: signed repository_dispatch ingress, GitHub-hosted Actions validation and live-PR anchoring, git-backed snapshot/event storage, GitHub Pages HTTPS serving, and hosted bad-signature failure. That path measures 0.690084 seconds for one dynamic Pages snapshot fetch plus 10,000 local reads and projects 2718x versus sampled repeated Pages point fetches, while the low-request control is 5.5512x. The public issue-ingress proof then removes the repository-write requirement from the request surface: a signed public GitHub issue triggers hosted validation, storage, and Pages serving; a bad-signature issue fails on the hosted runner; and the served artifact path projects 4327x while its low-request control is 8.8671x. The cross-repo client proof splits requester and service into separate public repositories: a hosted client workflow opens both signed and bad-signature service issues, waits for hosted issue workflows, verifies the served Pages snapshots, and still projects 3924x while the low-request control is 8.0606x. The raw-trace miner then attacks the hand-designed-substrate weakness: it generates join/index candidates from observed relation traces, selects `defines.file -> defines.symbol = refs.symbol -> refs.file` through training and heldout checks, includes discovery and validation oracle cost, and still reaches 1289x while its 20-repeat control is only 17.7109x. The real-git trace-mined fanout proof then moves that idea onto public LangChain history: 180 files, 5127 definition facts, 13050 reference facts, generated substrate selection on changed-file cases, content-addressed snapshots, and 5290x repeated-consumer fanout while the low-repeat control is 14.5735x. A public GitHub-hosted verifier then reruns that real-git mining stack on a clean Linux Actions runner against a fresh LangChain sparse clone, emits the report from hosted workflow logs, and reproduces 5290x with the low-request control still at 14.5735x. The hosted portfolio verifier then repeats the trace-mined proof across LangChain, Click, and HTTPX fresh public sparse clones; all three select the same generated substrate, the minimum speedup is 2019x, and the strongest low-request control remains only 16.3847x. The runtime-trace proof then moves beyond AST facts: executing unmodified public Click code under `sys.settrace` produced raw call events, mined a generated file-to-workflow index, validated heldout changed-file cases, and cleared 1629x locally. A public GitHub-hosted runner reproduced that runtime-trace result with 1971 call events, 1618.6917x speedup, and a 17.9467x low-request control. The upstream-pytest runtime-trace proof then removes the proof-authored-workflow weakness for this target: 24 upstream Click pytest cases produced 6591 raw call events locally, selected `events.file -> events.upstream_test_id`, and cleared 3840.7039x while the low-request control was 18.357x. A public GitHub-hosted runner reproduced the upstream-test result with 6535 call events, 24 tests, 3821.9354x speedup, and an 18.3602x low-request control. Point and bundle APIs fail the 1000x gate.
